lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there, Veldaban! Have you got any new quests for me?

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Not at the moment, no, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's too bad, I'm starting to get a little bored.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Then perhaps you should try your hand at the Hemenster Fishing Contest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, that's a bit of a random suggestion. Why would I go on a fishing contest?

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: We dwarves don't know much about fishing, but a couple of our brothers at White Wolf Mountain want to win the competition.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why would they want to do that if they don't know much about fishing?

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Ah, you see, the trophy is made of pure gold. And these dwarves in particular love gold.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, sounds interesting, I'll check it out. White Wolf mountain, you said?

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Yes, you can speak to a dwarf at either side of the mountain.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: [player name]! Have you heard?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean, half the world has suddenly become a sailor?

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: No, not that. It's the Red Axe!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What have those grumpy little guys done now?

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: They've left the city! They were already boycotting the trade floor, but now they've completely left the city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear, is that serious?

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: It's never happened before! At the moment it's unclear yet if they've left the Consortium as well. The directors are in turmoil!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what happens now?

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Well... I may require your services again. Are you interested in hearing more?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very interested!

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: We need some more information about what the Red Axe is up to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you asking me to... spy on them? Isn't that treason?

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: The other directors of the Consortium don't think so and are likely to expel the Red Axe completely anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where would you like me to go?

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: That's the problem. We don't know where they moved their headquarters to. But we may have one lead...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: There is a dwarf, all the way to the northeast in Keldagrim-East. He talks about kebabs a lot. Don't suppose you've met him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I do believe I have. Smashed glasses, wrecked house? Poor personal hygiene standards? Brother or cousin to that other drunken dwarf?

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: That's him, poor fellow. He didn't always used to be like that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I don't believe so.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: He lives in his brother's house.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Or was it his cousin?

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: In any case, he's a bit of a drunk these days. Well, when I say a bit, he's actually smashed all the time.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: But he seems to know something about the Red Axe for some odd reason. He speaks of them sometimes in one of his many rants.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Problem is, no one believes him. Everyone thinks he's completely mad. And perhaps they're right.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: But if he does indeed know something about the Red Axe or where their headquarters may be, we need to find out.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: What do you say then, [player name]? Do you want to help the Black Guard with this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like just the job for me!

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Excellent, good luck to you in your quest! May Guthix protect you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, here I am... at the drunken dwarf's relative's residence, who is also drunken. I wonder what drunken tales he can tell me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I try to stay away from drunken dwarves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Although I wish they would try to stay away from me too...

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Very well. Come back when you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Some other time, perhaps.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Very well.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: How goes the search for the Red Axe?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Haven't found them yet, sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to know about the Red Axe...

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: THE RED AXE WILL COME FOR YOU!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What, what do you mean?

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: I don't know... I c-c-c-can't think shtraight when I'm *hic* sober.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't look very sober to me!

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: DON'T contradict me! Or I won't tell you about ehm... about the... whatever it is you wanted to know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you ever considered to stop drinking? It's bad for you.

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: I'll shtop, right after you get me shome of the good shtuff!

sections.title: Talking to the drunken dwarf again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to know about the Red Axe...

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: Did you get me shome of the good stuff yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, here you go.

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: What wash that? *hic* No no no, this ish noooooo good! I want some of the REALLY good stuff.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the REALLY good stuff then?

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: Some of them original Kelda Stout! Ash blue ash the river! Ash strong ash ole Dondakan's rock. Ash foul tasting ash ash.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, er, I don't think that's good for you.

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: I know, but get me shome of it anyway. 'ere, have a seed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I supposed to do with a seed?

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: You silly human! Silly shilly silly *hic* hman. You need FOUR seeds, and grow them into hops.

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: Hop hop hop! *hic*

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I get the other seeds then?

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: Some of me drinking buddies might have 'em. They're quite rare, you shee. Oh yesh, quite rare.

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: Drinking buddies. Yes, find me some drinking buddies. No, find the other seeds. Yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I think what you're trying to say is... I need to find some other drunken dwarves, collect Kelda seeds from them until I have four, then grow them into hops so I can make Kelda stout.

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: You're awfully shmart for a human!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe that's just because I don't drink as much as you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, sorry.

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: Jusht pop down to the Laughing Miner and get some, will you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Laughing Miner?

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: It'sh the pub to the... er. North. No, shouth. South.

sections.title: Talking to the drunken dwarf again after giving him a beer

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to know about the Red Axe...

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: Got me my Kelda stout?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, no. I'm still trying to collect all of the seeds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm having some problems with the seeds...

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: Oh, you bothershome human. Here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, no. I need to grow my seeds into hops first.

sections.title: Talking to the Laughing Miner barmaid

lines.speaker: Barmaid
lines.text: Welcome to the Laughing Miner pub, human traveller.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like a Kelda stout please.

lines.speaker: Barmaid
lines.text: Sorry, we've stopped serving those. The seeds were too hard to get.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, tell me about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hold on a second there, I need your help!

lines.speaker: Rowdy dwarf
lines.text: Help, from me? You musht be more drunk than I am.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I try not to get drunk, it's not good for you. But I do need help in brewing some beer.

lines.speaker: Rowdy dwarf
lines.text: Mmmm, beer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to brew Kelda stout, but I haven't got enough seeds to grow the hops.

lines.speaker: Rowdy dwarf
lines.text: Mmmm, Kelda stout.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where I can find any seeds?

lines.speaker: Rowdy dwarf
lines.text: I might... oh yeah, I jusht might have a sheed like that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you're not going to just give it to me, are you?

lines.speaker: Rowdy dwarf
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright then, what do you want for it?

lines.speaker: Rowdy dwarf
lines.text: Lemme think... oh yesh. Get me... [item].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?? Where am I going to get that!

lines.speaker: Rowdy dwarf
lines.text: You can at least make the effort.

sections.title: Talking to the rowdy dwarf again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any Kelda seeds?

lines.speaker: Rowdy dwarf
lines.text: Where's my ehm... What wash it again? Oh yesh... [item].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, I've got it here!

lines.speaker: Rowdy dwarf
lines.text: Shplendid! Give that to me please!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now how about those seeds?

lines.speaker: Rowdy dwarf
lines.text: Well, I've got a Kelda hop seed right here! Pashed down generation after generation in my family...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, really?

lines.speaker: Rowdy dwarf
lines.text: Nah, I'm just kidding! Here, have the seed, you won't catch me farming in shome dirt patch anyway!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose you wouldn't ever have a proper job or do some decent hard work, no.

lines.speaker: Rowdy dwarf
lines.text: What wash that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still looking for that.

lines.speaker: Rowdy dwarf
lines.text: Look a little harder!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. I'm looking for some Kelda hop seeds.

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: Oh, we've got an expert here! Kelda shtout is delishious!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It sounds ghastly to me. But I want to brew some to help out, errr, a friend of mine.

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: Oh, I've got a sheed you might like to borrow!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Borrow?? How can I borrow a seed? I can hardly give it back once I've grown it.

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: Then we've got no deal!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What if I offer you a drink?

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: Ah, err, are you offering?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just thought I'd save myself the trouble of waiting for you to ask me this time.

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: I've never ashked you for a drink in my life!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, but it seems just about everyone else has!

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: Well it'sh hardly a big deal, ish it? We've got a huge shupply of dwarven shtouts right here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not the point!

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: And a dwarven shtout against a Kelda hop seed? I'm not sure if that'sh a fair trade.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dwarven stout, here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now do you want it or not? I'm not asking you again!

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: Alright, you talked me into it.

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: Hmm, that shtout was delicious... here, have my invaluable Kelda hop sheed now, it wash my lasht one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, well, I was going to offer you a dwarven stout, but I seem to have left it in my other pants.

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: Haha, you make me laugh human!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shtouts are just on the table over there, help yourshelf!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No wait, I want to borrow it after all.

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: You are going to give it back after you're done widdit, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course, of course! After I've grown my Kelda seeds into hops, then harvested the hops and brewed my Kelda stout... sure, I'll give you back your seed!

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: That'sh very deshent of you, human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Only too happy to help!

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: Hey, err... wait... wait a second! Hey, wait!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, yes?

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: How are you goin' to give me back my sheed once you've made the Kelda stout? You'll be too drunk to find your way back here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, don't worry... like I said, it's not for myself, it's for a friend.

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: That'sh alright then, here you go.

sections.title: Talking to Khorvak again before obtaining his seed

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still looking for some Kelda hop seeds.

lines.speaker: Khorvak, a dwarven engineer
lines.text: Oh, I've got a sheed you might like to borrow!

sections.title: Talking to the King's Axe Inn barman

lines.speaker: Barman
lines.text: Yes? Can I help you with something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like a Kelda stout please.

lines.speaker: Barman
lines.text: We don't serve that sort of brew here, this is a high class establishment!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry... perhaps you can tell me where to get some then?

lines.speaker: Barman
lines.text: If you insist. You might want to try the Laughing Miner in East. If anyone is selling that, it would be them.

lines.speaker: Barman
lines.text: But I doubt it. Kelda stout is very rare these days. Fortunately.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll check it out, thanks.

lines.speaker: Gauss
lines.text: A good day to you, sir!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And to you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Say, you know a lot about beer, right?

lines.speaker: Gauss
lines.text: A fair bit if I say so myself, yes. What do you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know anything about Kelda stout?

lines.speaker: Gauss
lines.text: Ah, very rare that is! Very rare indeed. This place doesn't stock it. Don't think the Laughing Miner does either.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, that's what I heard, so I'm going to brew it myself.

lines.speaker: Gauss
lines.text: Then you'll probably need some seeds!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you got any?

lines.speaker: Gauss
lines.text: Have a toast to our good health with me and I'll tell you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Drinking is bad for you, but I'll make an exception this time.

lines.speaker: Gauss
lines.text: Hurrah for the human!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cheers!

lines.speaker: Gauss
lines.text: Cheers!

lines.speaker: Gauss
lines.text: That was fun! Here, I've got one Kelda seed left. A sociable chap like you is more than welcome to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have anything suitable to toast with.

lines.speaker: Gauss
lines.text: Then go get something from the bar!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So did you have any Kelda seeds?

lines.speaker: Gauss
lines.text: Were you going to have a toast to our good health?

sections.title: Attempting to plant Kelda seeds before asking for permission

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better ask the gardener for permission to use this patch first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some help.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Yes, [player name]? What can a humble gardener like myself help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have four Kelda seeds here, but need some advice on growing them.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Marvelous, those seeds are quite rare don't you know!

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: First of all, you must remember that these seeds only grow underground. Sunlight is harmful to them, for some strange reason.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: There is a patch in the palace garden that you're more than welcome to use.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks! Is there anything else I need to know?

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Just take care of the weed in the patch and plant your seeds. They'll grow into hops very quickly, if you're lucky. Kelda seeds have a remarkably fast grow rate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you! Your advice is much appreciated!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some help.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Yes, [player name]? What can a humble gardener like myself help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I need some more help with this whole farming business.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Just get rid of the weeds in the patch, plant you seeds and wait for the hops to grow. The miracle of nature will do the rest!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do I have to wait here all this time then?

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Yes and no. Yes, you do have to wait, but no, you don't have to stay here. You can help out people in the city, for instance, there's always someone who needs help with an urgent job.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean the mining companies? I did jobs for them, they're not very profitable.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: No no, just people like shopkeepers and such, they're quite willing to teach you things in exchange for the jobs as well.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: But here, tell you what, I'll give you some seeds if you can deliver this letter to a friend in Falador.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do I have to do that? I just want to grow my hops and get on with my quest for Veldaban.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Oh no, you don't have to do it all, I just thought you wanted something to do while you were waiting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll deliver that letter for you.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Happy to hear that! His name is Elstan, he lives just south of Falador.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be right back!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd rather just sit here and stare at this patch, thanks.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Very well. That can be quite relaxing, actually, I often do it myself.

sections.title: Talking to Rind after offering to deliver his letter

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some help.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Yes, [player name]? What can a humble gardener like myself help you with?

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]! Have you been able to deliver that letter yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I haven't been to Falador yet, but I will soon.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Much appreciated, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I've decided not to deliver it after all.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Oh, I'm sad to hear that.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Oh well, I'll see if I can find someone else instead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, sorry, I lost your letter.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: That's a little clumsy, [player name]! Here, I've got a copy, please deliver this one instead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. Are you Elstan?

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: Why yes, I am. Have I become that well known?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I've got a letter here from your friend in Keldagrim.

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: Ah, you mean Rind? Great to hear from the little fellow again. Can I see it please?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Elstan
lines.text: Most enlightening. Please give him my regards next time you see him, I look forward to the next conference.

sections.title: Talking to Rind after delivering his letter

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some help.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Yes, [player name]? What can a humble gardener like myself help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've delivered your letter to Elstan, he told me to give you his regards.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Wonderful, that's just wonderful! Here, have these two seeds as a reward. They may not be Kelda hop seeds, but you should be able to grow a decent amount of herbs with them.

sections.title: Talking to Rind after finishing or refusing his letter delivery

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some help.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Yes, [player name]? What can a humble gardener like myself help you with?

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: How are you getting along with growing your seeds into hops, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, I'm not sure... let me check the patch.

sections.title: Attempting to compost the Kelda hops patch
When the Kelda hops are grown
Inspecting the fully grown Kelda hops
Speaking to Rind after harvesting the Kelda hops

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some help.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Yes, [player name]? What can a humble gardener like myself help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've grown my seeds into hops, but I have no idea what to do with them. Can you turn them into stout for me please?

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Haha, it doesn't quite work like that, I'm afraid!

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: You will have to bring your hops to a brewery.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where's the nearest brewery then?

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Oh, there's one in Keldagrim-East, upstairs in the Laughing Miner. They brew their own beer they do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How does it work though?

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: I'm sure someone there will be able to help you out, it's not my specialty you see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, thanks a lot!

sections.title: Speaking to Rind after losing the Kelda hops

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some help.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Yes, [player name]? What can a humble gardener like myself help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've grown my seeds into hops, but I seem to have mislaid them.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Ah! Fortunately I found some hops after your recent harvest. Here, I believe this belongs to you.

sections.title: Talking to the drunken dwarf after harvesting the Kelda hops

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to know about the Red Axe...

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: I want my Kelda shtout!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My hops still need fermenting, sorry.

sections.title: Attempting to add Kelda hops to a brewery other than the one in Keldagrim
Attempting to add Kelda hops to the vat prematurely
Adding Kelda hops to the vat
Speaking to Rind while the Kelda Stout is brewing

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some help.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Yes, [player name]? What can a humble gardener like myself help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm brewing my hops into Kelda stout, but it seems to be taking an awfully long time.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Just have patience, my young friend! Have a wander around town, see if anyone has jobs for you!

sections.title: When the Kelda Stout has finished brewing
Speaking to Rind after the Kelda Stout has finished brewing

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some help.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Yes, [player name]? What can a humble gardener like myself help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just so angry, my hops aren't fermenting at all. I want my Kelda stout!

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Now now, my young human friend! Are you sure about that?

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Perhaps you should go back to the brewery, I wouldn't be surprised if your stout has fermented by now.

sections.title: Turning the vat valve after the Kelda Stout is finished
Attempting to drink the Kelda Stout

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This stout seems absolutely vile and disgusting. Besides, I'm supposed to bring it to my drunken, kebab obsessed friend.

sections.title: Talking to the Laughing Miner barmaid after brewing Kelda Stout

lines.speaker: Barmaid
lines.text: Welcome to the Laughing Miner pub, human traveller.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like a Kelda stout please

lines.speaker: Barmaid
lines.text: They're quite hard to come by, and I believe you've already got one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes.

lines.speaker: Barmaid
lines.text: Here, I found a little left over Kelda stout, just enough for a glass. Take it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, for free?

lines.speaker: Barmaid
lines.text: I don't think I'd be able to sell it to anyone.

lines.speaker: Barmaid
lines.text: So yes, go ahead.

sections.title: Talking to the King's Axe Inn barman after brewing Kelda Stout

lines.speaker: Barman
lines.text: Yes? Can I help you with something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like a Kelda stout please.

lines.speaker: Barman
lines.text: They're not easy to come by... and I believe you already have one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, you're right.

sections.title: Talking to the drunken dwarf after collecting the Kelda Stout

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to know about the Red Axe...

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: I want my Kelda shtout!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Haven't got any on me, sorry.

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: Well hurry up! I'm dying of thirsht!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got some Kelda stout right here. I just brewed it in the Laughing Miner!

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: Now there'sh a good human, gimme that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure? This is pretty bad stuff.

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: You want to hear about the Red Kebabsh or what? I mean... the Red Axe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go then.

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: Ooooh, THAT'sh the good shtuff!

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: The Red Axe? You want to know about them, do you? Then let me tell you my tale. It is long and quite unforgettable...

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: A long time ago, quite a while ago, yes... I resigned from my mining company, the Magenta Accordion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The what? The Magenta Accordion? That's not a mining company!

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: It is, it is! It's one of the many minor companies, not part of the Consortium.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, if you say so...

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: Right, so I had just left my company. I'd had enough of slaving away in the mines and decided to become an explorer. Oh yes, like my cousin.

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: I explored this old mine, you know, just south of Keldagrim. I think it's boarded up now. Some amazing things I saw there.

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: First of all...

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: ...and then I nearly fell into the Abyss! But I...

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: ...so I had to run for my life, but then...

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: ...pink dragons...

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: ...and I don't really remember anything after that. People think I'm a drunken fool.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was very interesting! I'll ignore that part with the pink dragons though...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I shall investigate that tunnel immediately! South of the cart station, just south of the carts to White Wolf mountain?

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: If you shay sho... I just want to smash up shome shtuff now.

sections.title: Talking to the drunken dwarf again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to know about the Red Axe...

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: I don't know anyshing about the Red Kebabs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But that tale you told me!

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: I don't know what you're *hic* talking 'bout.

sections.title: Talking to Veldaban after hearing the drunken dwarf's story

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: How goes the search for the Red Axe?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'm on to something! Something about a disused mine tunnel southeast of town.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Happy to hear that, [player name]! Please go and check it out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me, can you tell me what's behind that boarded up tunnel to the south?

lines.speaker: Cart conductor
lines.text: Why, that part of the mines hasn't been used in a long time. I don't rightly know what you would find behind there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to go through there. Can you open up the tunnel for me?

lines.speaker: Cart conductor
lines.text: I don't have the authority to do that kind of thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then who do I talk to? I really need to get in.

lines.speaker: Cart conductor
lines.text: I don't know. Perhaps you have an influential friend?

sections.title: Talking to the cart conductor again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I really need to go through that tunnel.

lines.speaker: Cart conductor
lines.text: I can't help you, sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: At least tell me who can, please?

lines.speaker: Cart conductor
lines.text: I don't know that either. Don't you have any influential friends in the city?

sections.title: Talking to Veldaban after speaking to the cart conductor

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: How goes the search for the Red Axe?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I may have a good lead now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I need some boarding removed, I need to go through some disused mine cart tunnels.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: I see... I cannot make that decision, I'm afraid. Speak to the director of [company name], he may be able to help you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah yes, I'm a member now, they must be able to help!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Hello [player name]! Have you seen the statue? It looks beautiful! It's a pity it's been overshadowed by the departure of the Red Axe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me with a boarded up tunnel?

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Ah, and what would be lurking behind this particular tunnel?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think it leads to something that might be related to the Red Axe.

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: The Red Axe!

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: This is indeed an important issue. They've gone too far this time. A major company willingly leaving the Consortium has never happened before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me get the boarding removed then?

lines.speaker: [company name] Director
lines.text: Forthwith, [player name], forthwith! I'll order the removal at once!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks! I'll investigate the tunnel immediately!

sections.title: Talking to Veldaban after talking to the company director

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: And? What of the Red Axe?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will have lots to tell you soon, Veldaban!

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Happy to hear that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm deep underground now, but how am I supposed to get across here?

sections.title: Returning to Keldagrim on the cart
Attempting to control the dwarven machinery without any square stones
Riding the cart before the junction is switched

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This junction is blocked. I'd better go back to the start and find some way to make it go left or right.

sections.title: Attempting to drop a square stone
Riding the cart back to the start
Riding the cart to a dead end

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've come to a dead end. I'd better get back to the start.

sections.title: Listening in on the Red Axe

lines.speaker: Red Axe Henchman
lines.text: What's the new plan?

lines.speaker: Ogre shaman
lines.text: Me dead human!

lines.speaker: Red Axe Director
lines.text: No, not yet. He may still prove useful to us.

lines.speaker: Red Axe Henchman
lines.text: My axe on his head would be more useful!

lines.speaker: Red Axe Director
lines.text: Forget about the human and the statue for now. Our experiments are more important.

lines.speaker: Red Axe Henchman
lines.text: We are nearly ready with our next test.

lines.speaker: Ogre shaman
lines.text: More little fire lookers?

lines.speaker: Red Axe Director
lines.text: This time they'll survive, I hope.

lines.speaker: Red Axe Henchman
lines.text: Our slaves are too weak, it will never work.

lines.speaker: Red Axe Director
lines.text: Yes, things would have been so much easier with the statue.

lines.speaker: Red Axe Henchman
lines.text: It was a great plan! Sabotaging the base of the statue and bribing that boatman to crash into it!

lines.speaker: Red Axe Director
lines.text: And we would have gotten away with it too, if it wasn't for that pesky human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pesky human, huh? Well, I think I have enough info now. I'd better explore the rest of these mines.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the next room before the dialogue is finished

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should stay and listen for a bit longer...

sections.title: Overhead text when listening in on the Red Axe

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Looks like another chasm I need to cross.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the next room without inspecting everything

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should explore this room fully before moving on. I should be able to find out a little bit more.

sections.title: Searching the crate with administrative info

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Statistical data, salary structures, shopping lists... no, I don't think there's anything interesting here.

sections.title: Searching the crate with the boatman's letter
Searching other bookcases after finding the employee records

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What the Guthix is this? Another chasm? And even more tracks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's the Red Axe director again!

lines.speaker: Red Axe Director
lines.text: How did the experiment go?

lines.speaker: Colonel Grimsson
lines.text: The test subjects are still alive, director. They will suffice as soldiers.

lines.speaker: Red Axe Director
lines.text: Excellent! We are improving. Laneel!

lines.speaker: Red Axe Director
lines.text: Return to Arposandra and tell your superiors we've had a partial success.

lines.speaker: Gnome emissary
lines.text: I will tell them, but I'd like to see some of your test subjects first, if I may.

lines.speaker: Colonel Grimsson
lines.text: Look, here comes the first group!

lines.speaker: Red Axe Director
lines.text: Behold the power of Zamorak!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Chaos dwarves!!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I must tell Veldaban!!!

lines.speaker: Grunsh
lines.text: Lookings likes we's finding da spying creatures'ies!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nooooooo!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, what? Where am I? Errrrr... I must tell Veldaban... something. Yes... ehm. I don't remember what though. Errr...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Veldaban, I must tell you something incredibly important!

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: What is it, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I must tell you about... about...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...about my incredible craving for a kebab. Mmm, kebabs. A kebab and a beer, that sounds great right now.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Errr, are you alright? Don't you have any important information about the Red Axe?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mmm, yes, I'll be perfectly alright... just after I've had my beer and a kebab. Yes. I'll have a beer and a kebab in the Laughing Miner. Yum!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Watch out kebabs, I come for you!

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Poor [player name], I think you've been bewitched.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: [player name], I thought you had something important to tell me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes... yes... Red... ehm. Red...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Red Kebab? Red Ale?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will feast on kebabs and beer! Ahahaha!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I knew it were you matey! 'Ere, have some of the good stuff!

lines.speaker: Drunken Dwarf
lines.text: Very kind ob you, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Nolar
lines.text: Tell us another tale, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Factory Worker
lines.text: Tell us more about your kebabs!

lines.speaker: Gauss
lines.text: Haha, kebabs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, these kebabsh, right? *hic* A whole g-g-group of kebabs came marching in! I'm telling you! Marching in line!

lines.speaker: Gunslik
lines.text: Hahaha!

lines.speaker: Factory Worker
lines.text: Tee hee hee!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And then, I'll tell you... Then! Ehm...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then I'll have another drink!

lines.speaker: Nolar
lines.text: Hahaha!

lines.speaker: Factory Worker
lines.text: Hahaha, idiot!

lines.speaker: Gunslik
lines.text: Tee hee hee!

lines.speaker: Colonel Grimsson
lines.text: Ha, the spell has worked its magic again.

lines.speaker: Colonel Grimsson
lines.text: No one will believe that drunken fool now!

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: What happened, [player name], did you finish your assignment?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think... I think I must have. It's so hard to remember.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Please, try to!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have this terrible headache.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know, I think something terrible has happened, but everyone seems to think I'm an idiot for some reason...

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: Don't worry, [player name], I believe you. You must have been put under a spell of some sort to make you forget.

lines.speaker: Commander Veldaban
lines.text: I suppose we'll just have to wait and see what happens.
